title: Create router module with APIRouter, imports, and dependencies
description:
Part of parent issue BE-006: Build recipes router.

This unit only: create the file `app/api/routes/recipes.py` with the router scaffold — imports + APIRouter declaration + dependency closures. NO endpoint bodies yet (those land in U2-U6).

Required:
- `router = APIRouter(prefix='/recipes', tags=['recipes'])` (auto-mount adds `/api/v1`)
- Imports from FastAPI: APIRouter, Depends, HTTPException, status, Path
- Imports for SQLAlchemy: AsyncSession
- Imports from app: `require_roles` (from app.core.auth), `get_db` (from app.db.session), `User` (from app.models.user), `RecipeCreate`/`RecipeOut` (from app.schemas.recipe), `create_recipe`/`ensure_local_user` (from app.services.recipes)
- Capture the dependency closure once: `_require_user_or_admin = require_roles('user', 'admin')` — tests need a stable handle to override
- Module docstring describing what the file will contain across U1-U7

The file should be syntactically valid and importable after this unit, even with no endpoints.

title: Implement POST /api/v1/recipes (create)
description:
Part of parent issue BE-006: Build recipes router.

This unit only: add the POST `''` handler to `app/api/routes/recipes.py` (U1 already created the file with imports + router).

Handler:
```python
@router.post('', response_model=RecipeOut, status_code=status.HTTP_201_CREATED)
async def create_recipe_endpoint(
    payload: RecipeCreate,
    db: AsyncSession = Depends(get_db),
    user: User = Depends(_require_user_or_admin),
) -> RecipeOut:
    owner_id = user.user_id  # JWT-derived
    return await create_recipe(db, owner_id=owner_id, data=payload)
```

Key rules:
- owner_id from JWT (via `user.user_id` returned by `require_roles`), NEVER from payload
- RecipeCreate's `extra='forbid'` rejects client-supplied owner_id/id with 422
- 201 Created on success

title: Implement GET /api/v1/recipes/mine (list)
description:
Part of parent issue BE-006: Build recipes router.

This unit only: add the GET `/mine` handler. Register BEFORE `/{recipe_id}` so FastAPI's path matcher doesn't treat `mine` as a UUID.

Handler returns `List[RecipeOut]` of recipes where `owner_id == user.user_id`, ordered by `created_at DESC`. Use SQLAlchemy `select` + `where` + `order_by`. Returns `[]` when the caller has no recipes.

No `ensure_local_user` here — list is read-only, no FK insert.

title: Implement GET /api/v1/recipes/{recipe_id} (read)
description:
Part of parent issue BE-006: Build recipes router.

This unit only: add the GET-by-id handler.

Handler signature:
```python
@router.get('/{recipe_id}', response_model=RecipeOut)
async def get_recipe_endpoint(
    recipe_id: UUID,
    db: AsyncSession = Depends(get_db),
    user: User = Depends(_require_user_or_admin),
) -> RecipeOut:
    ...
```

Returns the recipe if `recipe_id` exists AND owner_id matches the caller. Otherwise 404 (`detail='recipe_not_found'`). Do NOT distinguish 'not found' from 'not yours' — both are 404 to avoid leaking existence.
success_criteria: 404 with detail='recipe_not_found' on miss or cross-owner

title: Implement PUT /api/v1/recipes/{recipe_id} (update)
description:
Part of parent issue BE-006: Build recipes router.

This unit only: add the PUT handler. Full-replacement semantics (PUT, not PATCH).

Handler:
```python
@router.put('/{recipe_id}', response_model=RecipeOut)
async def update_recipe_endpoint(
    recipe_id: UUID,
    payload: RecipeCreate,
    db: AsyncSession = Depends(get_db),
    user: User = Depends(_require_user_or_admin),
) -> RecipeOut:
    ...
```

Look up the recipe by (recipe_id, owner_id=user.user_id). 404 if not found OR not owned. Otherwise overwrite the mutable fields from payload (title/description/ingredients/instructions/prep_time_minutes/cook_time_minutes/servings). NEVER touch id/owner_id/created_at; updated_at gets the server time via the model's onupdate=func.now().
success_criteria: Does not mutate id, owner_id, created_at

title: Implement DELETE /api/v1/recipes/{recipe_id}
description:
Part of parent issue BE-006: Build recipes router.

This unit only: add the DELETE handler.

Handler:
```python
@router.delete('/{recipe_id}', status_code=status.HTTP_204_NO_CONTENT)
async def delete_recipe_endpoint(
    recipe_id: UUID,
    db: AsyncSession = Depends(get_db),
    user: User = Depends(_require_user_or_admin),
) -> None:
    ...
```

Look up by (recipe_id, owner_id=user.user_id). 404 if not found or not owned. Otherwise delete and return None (FastAPI emits 204 No Content with empty body). Idempotency note: second DELETE on the same id returns 404 — that's fine.
success_criteria: Returns None (204 No Content) on success

title: Add 422→400 coercion for malformed UUID path params
success_criteria: U4/U5/U6 handlers updated to use the dependency
Malformed UUID path param returns 400 (not 422) with detail='invalid_recipe_id'
